name: Ciabatta #2
recipeIngredient: 2 Cakes compressed fresh yeast (0.6 oz each)
1 3/4 cups Cold water; from the tap
1 tbsp Kosher salt or flaked sea salt
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, make a well in the center of the flour.
2. Crumble the fresh yeast into a small bowl and stir with 1/2 cup water until smooth.
3. Pour the yeast mixture into the well in the flour.
4. Add the remaining water to the well and mix.
5. Mix the flour from the bowl into the yeast mixture using your hand or wooden spoon to make a very sticky dough.
6. Beat the mixture with your hand for 5 minutes until very elastic. Cover with a damp dish towel and let rise at room temperature, away from drafts, for 4 hours until it rises and collapses.
7. Punch down the dough. Add oil and salt to the dough and mix briefly.
8. Gradually work in the rest of the flour into the dough using your hand to make a soft, sticky dough.
9. Cover with a damp dish towel and let rise at room temperature, away from drafts, until doubled in size (about 1 hour).
10. Using a very sharp knife, divide the dough in half.
11. Do not punch down or knead the dough. Shape each portion into rough rectangular loaves about 1 inch thick on prepared baking sheets.
12. Sprinkle the loaves with flour and let rise, uncovered at room temperature away from drafts, until doubled in size (45 minutes to 1 hour).
13. During the last 15 minutes of rising, preheat the oven to 425°F.
14. Bake the loaves for about 35 minutes or until they sound hollow when tapped underneath.
15. Transfer the loaves to wire racks until lukewarm, then serve or eat within 24 hours gently warmed. Freeze for up to one week only.
